title: Fracttalix AI Layer Schema v3
description: Schema for machine-readable falsification layers in the Fracttalix corpus. Implements the Dual Reader Standard (DRS). Version 3, Session 51. Adds per-claim verification tier, test bindings, verified_against SHA, and semantic_spec_url for Layer 0.

semantic_spec_url.description: URL to the falsification-kernel.md semantic specification (Layer 0). Defines predicate grammar and evaluation rules for all Type F claims.

paper_id.description: Fracttalix corpus paper ID. P1–P13 for FRM corpus; MK-P1–MK-P5 for Meta-Kaizen corpus.

claim_registry.description: All claims in the paper. Type A: axioms. Type D: definitions. Type F: falsifiable claims.

tier.description: Verification tier. axiom: foundational (Type A), unfalsifiable by design. definition: definitional (Type D), no predicate. software_tested: Type F claim with passing test bindings in this codebase. analytic: Type F verified by formal derivation trace only. empirical_pending: Type F with active placeholder or pending external data. formal_proof: Type F verified by step-indexed derivation table with n_invalid_steps=0.

test_bindings.description: Fully qualified pytest node IDs (file::Class::method) that exercise this claim. Empty array if no software tests.

verified_against.description: Git commit SHA at which test_bindings were last verified passing. Null if no software tests.

inference_rules.description: Canonical inference rule inventory for Fracttalix derivation traces.

rules.name: Modus Ponens
rules.description: If P and P→Q then Q.

rules.name: Universal Instantiation
rules.description: If ∀x P(x) then P(a) for any specific a.

rules.name: Substitution of Equals
rules.description: If a=b then any expression containing a may replace a with b.

rules.name: Definition Expansion
rules.description: Replace a defined term with its definition.

rules.name: Algebraic Manipulation
rules.description: Valid algebraic transformation preserving equality or inequality.

rules.name: Logical Equivalence
rules.description: Replace an expression with a logically equivalent one.

rules.name: Statistical Inference
rules.description: Apply a named statistical test or estimation procedure to data.

rules.name: Parsimony / Modeling Principle Selection
rules.description: Select a canonical value from a family of axiom-consistent options based on a named parsimony principle. Valid iff: (a) the family is established; (b) the principle is named and cited; (c) selection is documented as a canonical choice, not a necessary derivation.

rules.name: RG Fixed-Point Identification
rules.description: System at RG fixed point → universality class membership.

rules.name: Universality Class Identification
rules.description: Same fixed-point structure → same critical exponents.

rules.name: Perturbation Stability
rules.description: Fixed point stable under perturbation → exponents robust.

rules.name: Causal Precedence (DDE derivation class)
rules.description: For C1-C4 DDE system: Φ_τ forward-only → O(t'≤t) ⊥ x(t+τ). Derives from C1 (τ>0 makes Φ_τ non-invertible) via IR-4 (Definition Expansion). Not imported from external causal framework. First formalised DRP-8 Phase 0, HR-D8-0.
rules.session_added: IR-9/10/11: S42 (P2 build). IR-12: S54 (DRP-8 Phase 0).

rules.name: Protocol Specification
rules.description: A measurement protocol step is valid if: (a) it has a named input (observable, system parameter, or prior claim); (b) it specifies a deterministic operation (algebraic, statistical, or logical); and (c) it has a named output. A step requiring user judgment without a decision rule violates IR-13. First defined P3 S48 as local IR-12; renumbered to IR-13 S56 Phase 3 to resolve collision with canonical IR-12 (Causal Precedence).
rules.session_added: S48 (P3, as local IR-12). Canonical IR-13: S56 (P4 Phase 3, Objection 2 resolution).

rules.name: Statistical Standard Anchoring
rules.description: A statistical threshold is valid if anchored at a published statistical standard (named textbook or convention) OR derived from first principles. An arbitrary threshold violates Principle 10. First defined P3 S48 as local IR-13; renumbered to IR-14 S56 Phase 3 to resolve collision with canonical IR-12.
rules.session_added: S48 (P3, as local IR-13). Canonical IR-14: S56 (P4 Phase 3, Objection 2 resolution).
